title: eriogonum lonchophyllum var. nudicaule
intro: Eriogonum lonchophyllum var. nudicaule, also known as a desert buckwheat or cliff buckwheat, is a perennial wildflower that belongs to the Polygonaceae family and is native to the western United States and Canada. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations due to its antimicrobial and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Eriogonum lonchophyllum var. nudicaule include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and act as an antibacterial agent, making it a valuable remedy for various skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make salves, tinctures, and infusions, which can be applied topically to the affected area to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
